2604.13699.title: MIND: AI Co-Scientist for Material Research
2604.13699.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have enabled agentic AI systems for scientific discovery, but most approaches remain limited to textbased reasoning without automated experimental verification. We propose MIND, an LLM-driven framework for automated hypothesis validation in materials research. MIND organizes the scientific discovery process into hypothesis refinement, experimentation, and debate-based validation within a multi-agent pipeline. For experimental verification, the system integrates Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials, particularly SevenNet-Omni, enabling scalable in-silico experiments. We also provide a web-based user interface for automated hypothesis testing. The modular design allows additional experimental modules to be integrated, making the framework adaptable to broader scientific workflows. The code is available at: https://github.com/IMMS-Ewha/MIND, and a demonstration video at: https://youtu.be/lqiFe1OQzN4.
2604.13699.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）使得能夠進行科學發現的智能代理系統成為可能，但大多數方法仍然僅限於基於文本的推理，缺乏自動實驗驗證。我們提出了MIND，一個基於LLM的材料研究自動假設驗證框架。MIND將科學發現過程組織為假設精煉、實驗和基於辯論的驗證，並在多代理管道中進行。為了進行實驗驗證，系統整合了機器學習原子間勢，特別是SevenNet-Omni，實現可擴展的計算實驗。我們還提供了一個基於網頁的用戶界面，用於自動假設測試。模組化設計允許集成額外的實驗模組，使框架能夠適應更廣泛的科學工作流程。代碼可在此獲得：https://github.com/IMMS-Ewha/MIND，演示視頻可在此觀看：https://youtu.be/lqiFe1OQzN4。
2604.13699.author: Geonhee Ahn et.al.
2604.13699.authors: Geonhee Ahn, Donghyun Lee, Hayoung Doo, Jonggeol Na, Hyunsoo Cho, Sookyung Kim
